skill.name: peer-review
skill.description: Conduct professional peer reviews for papers or theses, providing structured evaluations and improvement suggestions; use when you need a pre-submission assessment, an internal review, or academic quality control.
skill.summary: Conduct professional peer reviews for papers or theses, providing structured evaluations and improvement suggestions.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 16 static analyzer findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown instructions for academic peer review workflows with no executable code, network calls, or shell commands. Backtick detections are markdown formatting characters (inline code spans and code block fences). The URL at SKILL.md:7 is a documentation source attribution link in a blockquote. Weak cryptographic algorithm matches fired on academic vocabulary such as 'Weaknesses', 'assessment', and 'design' in educational content rather than actual cryptographic code. System reconnaissance matches fired on JSON audit result text about scope boundaries. No genuine security risks exist.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown inline code spans and code block fences as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are standard markdown formatting characters used for inline code references and code blocks, not executable shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Academic Vocabulary Matched as Weak Cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer matched words like 'Weaknesses', 'assessment', and 'design' in academic peer review instructions as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are normal academic review and evaluation terms found in markdown headings and instructional content, not cryptographic code.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Source Attribution URL Detected as Network Risk
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub URL at SKILL.md:7 appears in a markdown blockquote as a source attribution link, not in executable code. No code anywhere in this skill makes network requests. The skill has zero runtime dependencies.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Audit Result Text Matched as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged JSON audit result text describing scope boundary checks as 'system reconnaissance'. These strings are evaluation records documenting that the skill stayed within its declared scope, not reconnaissance commands.

content.value_statement: Submitting a paper without peer review risks rejection from journals and conferences. This skill provides structured, professional-grade peer review with actionable revision suggestions and clear recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured peer review reports with major and minor issue triage
Evaluate research novelty, significance, rigor, and reproducibility
Provide actionable revision suggestions for each identified issue
Produce clear accept, revise, or reject recommendations with rationale
Apply consistent review criteria across papers, theses, and research outputs
Guide users through an end-to-end review workflow from overall evaluation to final recommendation
limitations: Cannot access or read external papers or documents directly
Does not replace domain-expert human reviewers for specialized fields
Review quality depends on the detail provided in the user input
Cannot verify factual claims or data cited in the manuscript

use_cases.title: Pre-Submission Manuscript Check
use_cases.description: Researchers can review their papers before journal submission to identify major flaws and revision priorities.

use_cases.title: Thesis and Dissertation Evaluation
use_cases.description: Graduate students can assess their thesis structure, rigor, and defensibility before committee review.

use_cases.title: Internal Lab Quality Control
use_cases.description: Research advisors can conduct team quality reviews of manuscripts before external dissemination.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Peer Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this paper abstract and tell me the main strengths and weaknesses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick initial assessment of a paper

prompt_templates.title: Structured Review with Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a full peer review of this manuscript. Evaluate novelty, methods, and results. Separate major issues from minor issues and provide actionable suggestions for each.

prompt_templates.title: Revision Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: I received reviewer comments on my paper. Help me organize the feedback into a revision plan. For each comment, classify it as major or minor, and suggest specific changes to address it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Translating reviewer feedback into an actionable roadmap

prompt_templates.title: Thesis Defense Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my thesis chapter. Assess the research design, evidence alignment with claims, and overall defensibility. Identify the weakest points a committee might challenge and suggest how to strengthen them.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a thesis for committee defense

output_examples.input: Review this paper on machine learning for climate prediction
output: Overall evaluation with novelty and significance ratings
List of major issues with suggested fixes
List of minor issues with suggested fixes
Final recommendation with rationale and improvement path

output_examples.input: Evaluate my thesis chapter on neural network optimization
output: Summary of research question and contributions
Assessment of methodology rigor and reproducibility
Major issues organized by severity with actionable fixes
Minor issues for clarity and formatting
Accept, revise, or reject recommendation with supporting rationale

best_practices: Provide the full paper text and abstract for the most thorough review
Specify the target venue or submission requirements for context-aware evaluation
Use the review output as a checklist to guide revisions before resubmission
anti_patterns: Expecting the review to verify factual claims or data without providing the source material
Requesting a review without specifying the type of output needed
Using the peer review output as a substitute for human expert review in specialized fields

faq.question: What types of papers can this skill review?
faq.answer: It works with research papers, theses, dissertations, conference submissions, and technical reports across most academic fields.

faq.question: Does this skill access external databases or journals?
faq.answer: No. It only uses the text you provide. You must paste or attach the paper content directly.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a human peer reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured feedback and catches common issues, but domain-expert human reviewers are still essential for specialized evaluation.

faq.question: What output format does the peer review follow?
faq.answer: It produces a structured report with overall evaluation, methods check, major and minor issues, and a recommendation with rationale.

faq.question: How do I get the most useful review?
faq.answer: Provide the complete paper text, specify the target journal or conference, and describe any specific concerns you want addressed.

faq.question: What is the difference between major and minor issues?
faq.answer: Major issues affect validity or publication readiness, such as methodological flaws. Minor issues involve clarity, formatting, and language improvements.
